title: BI-RADS 0 — Incomplete: need additional imaging
text: Incomplete: needs additional imaging evaluation and/or prior mammograms for comparison. Used at screening only when additional views or comparison studies are needed before final assessment.

title: BI-RADS 1 — Negative
text: Negative. Routine screening. No focal masses, distortions, or suspicious calcifications. Annual screening mammography (women age 40+).

title: BI-RADS 2 — Benign
text: Benign finding(s). Routine screening. Definitely benign findings (e.g. calcified fibroadenoma, secretory calcifications, fat-containing lesions). Annual screening.

title: BI-RADS 3 — Probably benign
text: Probably benign. Short-interval follow-up (6 months). Less than 2% likelihood of malignancy. Follow-up at 6, 12, 24 months.

title: BI-RADS 4 — Suspicious
text: Suspicious. Tissue diagnosis (biopsy). Subdivided 4A (low suspicion 2-10%), 4B (moderate 10-50%), 4C (high 50-95%). Biopsy recommended.

title: BI-RADS 5 — Highly suggestive of malignancy
text: Highly suggestive of malignancy. Tissue diagnosis + multidisciplinary care plan. > 95% likelihood of malignancy. Biopsy + oncology consultation.

title: BI-RADS 6 — Known biopsy-proven malignancy
text: Known biopsy-proven malignancy. Surgical excision when clinically appropriate. Pre-treatment staging or response monitoring.

title: Lung-RADS 1 — Negative
text: Negative. No nodules or definitely benign nodules. Continue annual screening LDCT.

title: Lung-RADS 2 — Benign appearance or behavior
text: Benign appearance or behavior. Nodules with very low likelihood of becoming clinically active cancer. Continue annual screening LDCT.

title: Lung-RADS 3 — Probably benign
text: Probably benign. 6-month LDCT follow-up. ~1% malignancy rate.

title: Lung-RADS 4A — Suspicious
text: Suspicious. 3-month LDCT follow-up or PET/CT or tissue sampling. ~5-15% malignancy rate.

title: Lung-RADS 4B — Very suspicious
text: Very suspicious. Tissue diagnosis (biopsy or surgical resection) +/- PET/CT. > 15% malignancy rate.

title: Lung-RADS 4X — Suspicious with additional features
text: 4A or 4B nodules with additional features suggesting malignancy (e.g. spiculated margins, growth). Same management as 4B.

title: LI-RADS LR-1 — Definitely benign
text: Definitely benign. 100% certainty of benign etiology (e.g. cyst, hemangioma). Routine follow-up per the indication for the original imaging.

title: LI-RADS LR-5 — Definitely HCC
text: Definitely hepatocellular carcinoma. 100% specificity in cirrhotic / high-risk patients. Multidisciplinary tumor board for treatment planning.

title: TI-RADS TR5 — Highly suspicious
text: Highly suspicious thyroid nodule (>= 7 points). Fine-needle aspiration if nodule >= 1 cm OR if growth, OR neck node finding. Annual surveillance if not biopsied and stable.

title: O-RADS US 5 — High risk of malignancy
text: High risk of malignancy (> 50%). Consultation with gynecologic oncology. Surgical management.

title: PI-RADS 5 — Very high (clinically significant cancer highly likely)
text: Very high (clinically significant prostate cancer highly likely). MRI-targeted biopsy. Length >= 1.5 cm or definite extra-prostatic extension / invasive behavior.
